title: acer truncatum subsp. quelpaertense
intro: Acer truncatum subsp. quelpaertense is a subspecies of the Acer truncatum tree, commonly known as the Quelpaert apricot or Korean maple, native to the Jeju Island in South Korea. It is a deciduous tree known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and has been shown to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels and improving cardiovascular health. In traditional Korean medicine, Acer truncatum subsp. quelpaertense is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various ailments, and its extract is also used in modern medicine for its potential therapeutic effects.
